data.title: Epsilon Energy divests Dewey Energy Holdings and assets in Anadarko basin
data.description:
• Epsilon Energy (EPSN) on Thursday announced the divestment of its wholly owned subsidiary, Dewey Energy Holdings, which holds the company’s Western Anadarko basin assets, to an undisclosed private buyer.
  • The assets sold included about 813 Mcfe/d (60% natural gas) of production (Q3 2025 figure) and approximately 6,400 net deep acres and 2,200 net shallow acres of leasehold, all located in Dewey County, Oklahoma, the company said.
  • The purchase price was $2.5 million in cash.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: RH Non-GAAP EPS of $1.71 misses by $0.45, revenue of $883.81M in-line
data.description:

  • Adjusted Operating Margin of 11.6% to 11.9%

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Costco logs FQ1 total comparable sales of 6.4%
T-Mobile authorizes new shareholder return program of up to $14.6B
data.description:
T-Mobile US (TMUS) announced a new shareholder return program of up to $14.6 billion that is expected to consist of additional repurchases of shares of the company’s common stock, and payment of cash dividends.

The telecom giant said the program will run through December 31, 2026.

According to a filing, the amount available under the shareholder return program for share repurchases will be reduced by the amount of any cash dividends declared and paid by the company, including the first quarter cash dividend of $1.02 per share payable on March 12, 2026.

The shareholder return program is in addition to the company’s $14 billion shareholder return program announced in December 2024 that runs through December 31, 2025, T-Mobile said, adding that any remaining unusedamount under the 2025 program shall be added to the 2026 program.

The company expects that the share repurchases and any dividends declared and paid to be made from available cash on hand andproceeds of one or more debt issuances or other borrowings.

data.title: Destination XL Group and FullBeauty Brands to combine in merger
data.description:
• Destination XL Group (DXLG), the retailer of Big + Tall men’s clothing and footwear, and FBB Holdings said on Thursday that they have entered into a definitive agreement to combine in a merger of equals. 
  • The combined company will unite complementary brands, channels, and capabilities to better serve plus-size women and Big + Tall men as a more flexible public company. 
  • The combined company will reflect a direct-to-consumer mix at 73% of total sales, with bricks-and-mortar at 27% of total sales.
  • The companies delivered combined net sales of approximately $1.2 billion for the last twelve months ending October 2025. 
  • Assuming no pro forma adjustments, adjusted EBITDA was approximately $45 million. Including $25 million in expected annual run-rate cost synergies, the combined company would have generated approximately $70 million of LTM Adjusted EBITDA.
  • Following the completion of the all-stock transaction, FullBeauty and DXL shareholders will own 55% and 45% of the combined company, respectively.
  • Following the closing of the merger, Jim Fogarty, current CEO of FullBeauty, will serve as Chief Executive Officer of the combined company, and Peter Stratton, current CFO of DXL, will serve as Chief Financial Officer. 
  • Headquarters will remain in Canton, MA, and the combined company is expected to maintain a significant presence in New York City, Indianapolis, and El Paso.
  • Under the terms of the merger agreement, FullBeauty will merge with a newly formed subsidiary of DXL, with DXL remaining the publicly traded entity under the ticker symbol DXLG. 
  • Destination XL Group (DXLG) shares were down more than 15% in post-market trading.

data.title: Amcor to implement 1-for-5 reverse stock split
data.description:
• Amcor (AMCR) on Thursday said it will proceed with the 1-for-5 reverse stock split.
  • Amcor expects to file an amendment to its memorandum of association to effect the reverse stock split after the close of trading on January 14, 2026.
  • Amcor ordinary shares will begin trading on a split-adjusted basis on January 15, 2026.
  • Amcor's CHESS Depositary Interests will also be consolidated on a 1-for-5 basis such that one CDI continues to represent an interest in one Amcor ordinary share following the reverse stock split.
  • The reverse stock split will reduce the number of outstanding ordinary shares from approximately 2.3 billion to approximately 461 million. 
  • Amcor ordinary shares will continue trading on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol "AMCR" and CDIs will continue to trade on the Australian Stock Exchange under the symbol "AMC".
  • Amcor intends to present its fiscal 2026 second quarter and second quarter year to date per share metrics, including earnings per share, on a split-adjusted basis when reported in early February 2026.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Badger Meter announces key executive leadership appointments
data.description:
• Badger Meter (BMI) announced on Thursday a series of executive leadership changes effective January 1, 2026.
  • Robert A. Wrocklage, who has served as Senior Vice President – Chief Financial Officer for seven years, will be elevated to Executive Vice President—North America Municipal Utility. 
  • Daniel R. Weltzien will be promoted to Vice President – Chief Financial Officer and Treasurer, following seven years as Vice President - Controller. 
  • Kimberly K. Stoll will be promoted to Vice President – Customer Support and General Manager – SmartCover. Matthew L. Stuyvenberg will be promoted to Executive Vice President - SaaS, Global Commercial and International Utility. 
  • Christina M. Tarantino will succeed Mr. Weltzien as Vice President - Controller. Corey T. Williams will transition from General Manager of SmartCover to Senior Director – Business Development. 
  • Management further reaffirmed its five-year forward outlook of high-single-digit organic revenue growth, normalized gross margin range of 39-42% and stated capital allocation priorities.
